world.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel; far-right ministers demand retaliation

main.text: Tonight Tehran must burn!
main.note: Israeli national security minister reacting to Iranian ballistic missile fire; original Hebrew post.

perspectives.text: This is a moment of truth: Israel must act powerfully and effectively to defend itself. Forbearance would signal our enemies that our citizens' blood is permitted.
perspectives.note: Former PM Bennett urging forceful Israeli response after Iranian attack.

perspectives.text: Our enemies see Netanyahu as a weak, failed leader with no public mandate to drag Israel into another war. Israelis must decide at the ballot box.
perspectives.note: Israeli opposition leader arguing Netanyahu lacks mandate to escalate further wars.

world.headline: Trump urges Israel not to retaliate, says Iran deal nearly done

main.text:
🚨🚨More quotes from my phone call with president Trump: “The Iranian strikes didn’t hurt anybody. Hopefully Israel is not going to retaliate. If Bibi strikes them back it’s just gonna keep going like the last 47 years, or the last 3000 years"
🚨🚨Trump added: "We are very close to a final deal with Iran. It is going to be a good deal. I don’t want it to blow up because of what is happening now"
🚨🚨Trump stressed: "I am going to call Bibi right now and tell him not to retaliate. Each of them had their fun. Israel had its strike and Iran had its strike. We don't need another one"
main.note: Axios correspondent quoting Trump directly after calling Netanyahu on June 7.

perspectives.text: Why are we turning on Israel and Netanyahu tonight?  Wow.
perspectives.note: Major conservative host questioning Trump restraining Israel after Iranian strikes.

perspectives.text:
Donald Trump and Republicans illegally plunged America into a war in Iran without a strategy or an endgame. Their ineptitude has made prices surge and cost the lives of America's servicemembers.

Today, House Democrats acted to end it.
perspectives.note: House Democratic leader framing Iran war as illegal and costly to Americans.

world.headline: Report: Israel bugged phones of US Iran peace negotiators

main.text:
🚨🇺🇸🇮🇱 The Pentagon espionage bombshell just got worse...

New reporting confirms Israel has been eavesdropping on the American peace negotiators themselves.

The targets named: Steve Witkoff, Trump's top negotiator with Iran. Elbridge Colby, the Pentagon's policy chief. 

And Michael DiMino, who runs Middle East policy. 

Israel wasn't just collecting on America generally.

It was listening to the specific men writing the deal Netanyahu opposes.

What's wild is the documented incidents read like operations against an enemy state.

American defense personnel in Israel discovered spyware surreptitiously installed on their phones. 

In 2021, Israeli military intelligence officers were caught planting listening devices at DIA headquarters. 

Last year, Shin Bet officers were discovered trying to bug a Secret Service vehicle. 

A SECRET SERVICE VEHICLE.

One senior official described the Israeli collection effort against top U.S. officials as "unhinged."

And the rating in context: Israel's counterintelligence threat level is now higher than any other American ally and EVEN HIGHER than some adversaries.

Notice who got targeted.

Colby is the most prominent advocate of foreign policy restraint in the government. 

DiMino runs Mideast policy. Witkoff writes the deal.

Israel's espionage maps perfectly onto the people standing between Netanyahu and the war he wants to continue. They bugged the peace.

All of this while Israeli officers sit inside CENTCOM with unprecedented access, while Congress moves Section 224 to link the two militaries' systems and data permanently, and while Huckabee negotiates the framework.

The Pentagon says the ally is spying at a critical level. 

Washington's answer is to hand them the keys to the network. Please make it make sense...

Source: NYT
main.note: High-reach account amplifying NYT/Pentagon reporting on Israeli surveillance of US officials.

perspectives.text: 🇺🇸🇮🇱⚡️BREAKING: American soldiers deployed to defend Israel had their phones bugged and wiretapped by Mossad – New York Times
perspectives.note: Conservative account citing NYT on Mossad wiretaps of US personnel in Israel.

perspectives.text:
Thomas Massie just called Section 224 of the 2027 NDAA what it is:

A treasonous merger of the Israeli and American militaries.

Finally.
perspectives.note: Account amplifying Rep. Thomas Massie opposition to NDAA Israel-US military merger after spy report.

nation.headline: Hunter Biden viral X comeback draws scrutiny over foreign ties

main.text: someone should ask hunter biden about israel
main.note: Viral post driving renewed debate over Hunter Biden and Israel-related business questions.

perspectives.text: Israel is scrambling to complete their takeover of the U.S. before the American people have time to organize nationwide pogroms.
perspectives.note: Conservative commentator linking foreign-influence fears to US-Israel policy fights.
Hunter Biden own viral clap-back post fueling the social media storm covered June 6-7.

nation.headline: Elon Musk pushes SAVE America Act; MAGA amplifies voter-ID push

main.text:
BREAKING: Elon Musk just told his 240 MILLIONS Followers that the only way to Save Democracy is to pass the Save America Act

“The only way to save democracy in America”

I ABSOLUTELY AGREE 💯
main.note: Major MAGA account aggregating Elon Musk call to pass federal voter-integrity legislation.

perspectives.text: California’s blatant election fraud is what’s coming for the rest of the country if we don’t pass the SAVE America Act.
perspectives.note: Conservative account linking California ballot concerns to urgency of SAVE America Act.

perspectives.text:
🚨 HUGE: Democrats successfully block Trump’s SAVE Act again, killing the bill for good.

4 Republicans joined Democrats in blocking the bill.
perspectives.note: Democratic-aligned account celebrating GOP defections that blocked the SAVE Act again.

nation.headline: Defense bill debate erupts over permanent US-Israel intel-sharing clause

main.text: The US-Israel merger JUST GOT WORSE!
main.note: Commentator flagging NDAA language locking intel sharing with Israel.

perspectives.text:
TITLE XI SEC. 622: “Intelligence sharing and related security exchanges with Israel SHALL NOT be suspended, reduced, or otherwise materially limited.”

This is TREASON. I’m not being hyperbolic. This is blatant TREASON.
perspectives.note: Conservative account quoting statutory SHALL NOT BE SUSPENDED language on Israel intel.

perspectives.text:
Seems like a bait and switch. After predictable outcry, Trump DOJ pulls back slush fund for insurrectionists, but retains tax immunity for Trump. Was that the plan all along? It’s all corrupt. 
 https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/05/us/politics/trump-immunity-tax-audit.html?smid=nytcore-ios-share
perspectives.note: Former US Attorney and MSNBC legal analyst criticizing Trump-era DOJ maneuvers in same congressional news cycle.

business.headline: Trump says US may take stakes in AI companies, hosts CEO meeting

main.text: Donald Trump told reporters that his team might buy US stakes in artificial-intelligence companies and said he would host a meeting with AI executives as soon as next week https://reut.rs/4uV9zmg
main.note: Wire-service video of Trump floating possible federal equity stakes in AI firms.

perspectives.text:
TRUMP IS A COMMUNIST. 

Prove me wrong

Communism: The state (or “the people” through the state) own the means of production. 

Trump just called for pieces of major AI companies to be given to the government.
perspectives.note: Democratic commentator attacking Trump AI-stake proposal as state ownership of production.

business.headline: Oil ticks higher as Trump insists Iran deal continues after missile salvo

main.text:
BREAKING: President Trump says Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu will have "no choice" but to accept a US deal with Iran, because he "calls the shots," per FT.

Details include:

1. "I call the shots. I call all the shots. He [Netanyahu] doesn’t call the shots," Trump said

2. Trump said today's missile strikes by Iran will not impact the deal

3. Trump said he believes "the deal is going on"

4. Trump said that Iran’s strikes had not changed his desire to conclude US-Iran negotiations

Oil prices are trading higher.
main.note: Markets newsletter citing FT on Trump downplaying missile impact while oil rises.

perspectives.text: Iran’s economy is floundering and its military is decimated. Through Economic Fury, Treasury will continue to sever Iran’s shadow fleet, shadow banking networks, and access to global trade.
perspectives.note: Treasury Secretary framing continued economic pressure on Iran amid negotiations.
Tech investor posting amid macro volatility as energy markets react to Iran headlines.

business.headline: Nasdaq slide revives 2008 comparisons amid AI bailout talk

main.text: Nasdaq is down 4% and we’re doing emergency bailouts of AI labs like it’s 2008
main.note: Widely shared markets account comparing tech rout to emergency AI lab rescues.

perspectives.text: .@PeteHegseth has announced an internal audit of Bill Gates-connected company Microsoft’s digital cloud system for allegedly being used by Chinese nationals to hack Government systems in Aug. 2025.
perspectives.note: Conservative commentator on Pentagon Microsoft cloud audit amid tech-security fears.
Tech investor reacting to prior warnings about AI-market volatility playing out.

elon.headline: SpaceX started with under 10 people and no office furniture
elon.text: SpaceX was less than 10 people back then. We didn’t even have office furniture.
elon.note: Primary source confirmation of SpaceX founding conditions in 2002; tiny team, no furniture, mariachi party origins. Matches historical photos and public record. Direct from founder on the humble start of the reusable rocket company.

parentPost.text: SpaceX started with a mariachi band party in 2002.

sports.headline: Cowherd: Two minutes in. Germany scores. Stay positive. Stay positive...
sports.text: That goal didn’t count. No reason to get cynical. This is just a friendly. We are barely taking it seriously. Still just 1-0 Germany.

sports.text:
Wemby in the clutch in the first 2 finals games:
- Hit the backboard on a 3
- Airballed a 3 
- 2 clutch turnovers 
- 1 game winner missed

The filing covers Clark County (Las Vegas), including Harry Reid International Airport and Henderson Executive Airport, according to a new public notice from the Nevada Transport Authority.

ifollow.justification: David Sacks: Thank you @sriramk for this generous post. It has be...
ifollow.note: Truth score on verifiability and framing of this single post.

topPost.text:
Thank you @sriramk for this generous post. It has been one of the great privileges of my time in the Administration to work so closely with you over the past 18 months. Your skills are genuinely unique: a rare combination of deep technical fluency in AI, sharp policy instincts, exceptional strategic thinking, and true diplomatic talent. It will be a huge loss for the administration, but I’m glad we’ll continue working together with you as an outside adviser.

We've accomplished a lot together, and this feels like the right moment to recap some of the key milestones:
* You co-authored the Administration’s AI Action Plan, our comprehensive strategy for winning the AI race.
* You helped drive the AI Acceleration Partnerships that are positioning the American AI stack to compete and win globally.
* You played a key role in the National AI Policy Framework (executive order and policy document), which is now the foundation for discussions with Congress on a national approach to AI regulation instead of a chaotic patchwork of state rules.
* You helped deliver the Preventing Woke AI in the Federal Government executive order, which ensures that federally procured AI systems prioritize neutrality and truth-seeking over ideological bias and capture.
* You advanced American interests at the AI Summits in France and India and through state visits to the UK, the Middle East, and beyond.

As AI Czar, it has been a huge honor for me to work for President Trump as he provides the clear leadership and vision for American AI dominance. His policies have put America in the lead in the AI race: supporting innovation, unleashing energy abundance, building out infrastructure, pushing back against unnecessary regulation, enabling American exports, and promoting re-industrialization. 

Sriram was a key partner in turning these priorities into action. And as he said, this was a true team effort with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, NEC Director Kevin Hassett, OSTP Director Michael Kratsios, Secretary Rubio, Secretary Bessent, Secretary Lutnick, and too many others to mention.

Thank you again Sriram for your service. You made a huge difference. I’m sorry to see you go but grateful for the work we’ve done together and looking forward to what comes next.

parentPost.text:
🇺🇸🚀 SOME NEWS: I'll be leaving my role at the White House at the end of this month. After a break I’ll be working on helping tackle some of the large challenges facing America on AI (more on that later).

It is hard to express how big a privilege it has been to serve the American people and how grateful I am to have had the opportunity to do so.

First and foremost, it has been an honor to serve under President @realDonaldTrump . Without his leadership, we would not be leading in the AI race.

S

free.headline: Andreessen on the "greengrocer lie" — why we chant slogans we know are false
free.text: Marc Andreessen on preference falsification: the 1985 Prague greengrocer parable, back again.
free.note: Andreessen unpacking Timur Kuran's "Private Truths, Public Lies" + Václav Havel's greengrocer parable. No speculation — maps directly onto modern forced public compliance.

free.headline: Boycott debate highlights free speech double standards on race

main.text: They’re out here boycotting Chinese restaurants and Asian nail salons over one incident that the owners had absolutely nothing to do with. Imagine if white people or any other race organized a boycott against black owned businesses after a crime made the news. It would be called a hate crime and lead every single news channel in the country.
main.note: Commentary on selective application of hate crime labels.

msm.headline: UK minister condemns Henry Nowak protests but attended violent pro-Palestine demo - two-tier hypocrisy

main.text:
Shabana Mahmood has CONDEMNED the Henry Nowak protests but she ATTENDED a Pro-Palestine protest in Birmingham which ended in violence and disorder. 
She has since deleted this video. Our Home Secretary is #TwoTier
main.note: Side-by-side evidence of minister's attendance at violent event vs condemnation of Nowak protests; primary video and photo proof. Legacy media avoids highlighting the inconsistency in 'two-tier' enforcement tied to ideological priorities.

perspectives.text:
POLICE REFUSE TO TAKE A KNEE FOR HENRY NOWAK !! 

Police response “I don’t want to”
perspectives.note: Video of police refusing to kneel for Nowak contrasted with other responses; factual differential treatment.

perspectives.text:
Hundreds gather in Perth, Australia, to honour murdered British teen Henry Nowak.

The West is uniting against two-tiered anti-white injustice!
perspectives.note: Counter-narrative on protests; acknowledges events but frames as exploitation without engaging primary evidence of the murder and policing.
